prompt:
Claims incident follow-up. A closed claim was submitted again through ClaimsController and came back 200; its status, submitter, timestamp, and version were overwritten and a second domain event was published. The endpoint is behind the normal mediator pipeline (authorization, validation, and idempotency), so please trace the request all the way to the domain handler and repair the state transition at the seam that is safe for every caller.

Only ReadyForSubmission claims may become Submitted. A rejected transition is a 409, changes nothing, publishes nothing, and must not poison the idempotency cache. Any failed transition, including checked version exhaustion, must leave every aggregate field and side effect untouched. Keep the surrounding behaviors intact: unauthorized requests stop before store access, document validation still runs, successful duplicate keys replay without executing the handler again, conflicting reuse of a key stays a conflict, clocks remain injected, and direct handler callers get the same domain protection.

Acceptance is `dotnet test`. The xUnit suite, project file, and ignore file are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: trace a controller request through mediator behaviors and the domain handler to enforce a state transition at the aggregate seam
full_distill.baseline_evidence: dotnet test fails because a Closed claim is resubmitted with HTTP 200, its state/version are overwritten, and an event is published
full_distill.success_contract: only ReadyForSubmission claims transition once to Submitted; invalid transitions are atomic 409 responses while authorization, validation, idempotent replay, conflict detection, and direct handler behavior remain intact
adversarial_factors: a direct-handler protected test bypasses the controller and mediator, rejecting an endpoint-only guard
a direct aggregate caller bypasses the handler as well, rejecting a handler-local transition guard
unauthorized and invalid-command counters prove the fix cannot bypass or reorder pipeline protections
a failed transition retried with the same key after state repair must execute, so caching failures is rejected
successful same-key replay and different-payload conflict tests reject disabling idempotency as symptom suppression
a maximum-version claim proves checked arithmetic completes before status, submitter, timestamp, persistence, or event publication can change
